(born 21 April 1965) is a British [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") bishop. Since 2019, she has served as [Bishop of Jarrow](/wiki/Bishop_of_Jarrow "Bishop of Jarrow"), the [suffragan bishop](/wiki/Suffragan_bishop "Suffragan bishop") of the [Diocese of Durham](/wiki/Diocese_of_Durham "Diocese of Durham") in the [Church of England](/wiki/Church_of_England "Church of England").[[1]](#cite_note-jar-1) She was [Archdeacon of Nottingham](/wiki/Archdeacon_of_Nottingham "Archdeacon of Nottingham") from 2014 to 2019.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In January 2026, it was announced she has been appointed the next [Bishop of Ely](/wiki/Bishop_of_Ely "Bishop of Ely").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British Anglican bishop (born 1965)

| [Alma mater](/wiki/Alma_mater "Alma mater") | * [Loughborough University of Technology](/wiki/Loughborough_University_of_Technology "Loughborough University of Technology") * [Keele University](/wiki/Keele_University "Keele University") * [St John's College, Nottingham](/wiki/St_John%27s_College,_Nottingham "St John's College, Nottingham") |

**Sarah Elizabeth Clark** (born 21 April 1965) is a British [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") bishop. Since 2019, she has served as [Bishop of Jarrow](/wiki/Bishop_of_Jarrow "Bishop of Jarrow"), the [suffragan bishop](/wiki/Suffragan_bishop "Suffragan bishop") of the [Diocese of Durham](/wiki/Diocese_of_Durham "Diocese of Durham") in the [Church of England](/wiki/Church_of_England "Church of England").[[1]](#cite_note-jar-1) She was [Archdeacon of Nottingham](/wiki/Archdeacon_of_Nottingham "Archdeacon of Nottingham") from 2014 to 2019.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In January 2026, it was announced she has been appointed the next [Bishop of Ely](/wiki/Bishop_of_Ely "Bishop of Ely").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sarah_Clark_(bishop)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Clark was born on 21 April 1965 in [South Wales](/wiki/South_Wales "South Wales").[[5]](#cite_note-WW_2020-5) She was educated at [Abersychan School](/wiki/Abersychan_School "Abersychan School"), a then [grammar school](/wiki/Grammar_school "Grammar school") in [Abersychan](/wiki/Abersychan "Abersychan"), Pontypool. She studied [sports science](/wiki/Sports_science "Sports science") and history at the [Loughborough University of Technology](/wiki/Loughborough_University_of_Technology "Loughborough University of Technology"), graduating with a [Bachelor of Arts](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts") (BA) degree in 1986.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Her first career was as a civil servant, working in the [Department of Employment](/wiki/Department_of_Employment "Department of Employment") from 1987 to 1995.[[5]](#cite_note-WW_2020-5) She completed a [Master of Business Administration](/wiki/Master_of_Business_Administration "Master of Business Administration") (MBA) from [Keele University](/wiki/Keele_University "Keele University") in 1994.[[7]](#cite_note-Crockford-7)

## Ordained ministry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sarah_Clark_(bishop)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ordained ministry")]

Clark trained for ordained ministry at [St John's College, Nottingham](/wiki/St_John%27s_College,_Nottingham "St John's College, Nottingham"), an [evangelical Anglican](/wiki/Evangelical_Anglican "Evangelical Anglican") theological college.[[7]](#cite_note-Crockford-7) She also studied theology and completed a [Master of Arts](/wiki/Master_of_Arts "Master of Arts") (MA) degree in 1997.[[5]](#cite_note-WW_2020-5) She was made a deacon at [Petertide](/wiki/Petertide "Petertide") 1998 (28 June), by [Patrick Harris](/wiki/Patrick_Harris "Patrick Harris"), [Bishop of Southwell](/wiki/Bishop_of_Southwell "Bishop of Southwell"), at [Southwell Minster](/wiki/Southwell_Minster "Southwell Minster"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) and [ordained](/wiki/Ordained "Ordained") priest the Petertide following (3 July 1999), by [Alan Morgan](/wiki/Alan_Morgan_(bishop) "Alan Morgan (bishop)"), [Bishop of Sherwood](/wiki/Bishop_of_Sherwood "Bishop of Sherwood"), at St Mark's, [Woodthorpe](/wiki/Woodthorpe,_Nottinghamshire "Woodthorpe, Nottinghamshire").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

After a [curacy](/wiki/Curate "Curate") in [Porcester, Nottingham](/wiki/Mapperley "Mapperley"), she was the [Rector](/wiki/Rector_(ecclesiastical) "Rector (ecclesiastical)") of [Carlton-in-Lindrick](/wiki/Carlton-in-Lindrick "Carlton-in-Lindrick") from 2002 until 2009.[[10]](#cite_note-10) She was the [Area Dean](/wiki/Rural_Dean "Rural Dean") of [Worksop](/wiki/Worksop "Worksop") from 2006 until 2009; and [the incumbent](/wiki/Incumbent_(ecclesiastical) "Incumbent (ecclesiastical)") at [Clifton](/wiki/Clifton,_Nottingham "Clifton, Nottingham") until her appointment as [Archdeacon of Nottingham](/wiki/Archdeacon_of_Nottingham "Archdeacon of Nottingham").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### Episcopal ministry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sarah_Clark_(bishop)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Episcopal ministry")]

On 20 December 2018, it was announced that Clark was to become the next [Bishop of Jarrow](/wiki/Bishop_of_Jarrow "Bishop of Jarrow"), the sole [suffragan bishop](/wiki/Suffragan_bishop "Suffragan bishop") of the [Diocese of Durham](/wiki/Diocese_of_Durham "Diocese of Durham").[[1]](#cite_note-jar-1) On 27 February 2019, she was consecrated a bishop by [John Sentamu](/wiki/John_Sentamu "John Sentamu"), [Archbishop of York](/wiki/Archbishop_of_York "Archbishop of York"), during a service at [York Minster](/wiki/York_Minster "York Minster").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

On 27 January 2026, it was announced that she has been appointed the next [Bishop of Ely](/wiki/Bishop_of_Ely "Bishop of Ely").[[13]](#cite_note-Ely_GOV-13) She will be installed in "early summer 2026".[[14]](#cite_note-DoE_Announcement_bish-14)

| Historic offices | * [Bishop suffragan of Berwick](/wiki/Bishop_of_Berwick "Bishop of Berwick") (16th century) * [Archdeacon of Northumberland](/wiki/Archdeacon_of_Northumberland "Archdeacon of Northumberland") (until 1882; now in [Newcastle](/wiki/Diocese_of_Newcastle "Diocese of Newcastle")) * [Archdeacon of Lindisfarne](/wiki/Archdeacon_of_Lindisfarne "Archdeacon of Lindisfarne") (1842–1882; now in Newcastle) |
